claim: GPT-4.1 adds a 1 million token context window, matching Gemini models; most API calls use <100k tokens so this is a catch-up, not a breakthrough
actionable: When evaluating million-token context claims, test if your actual use case needs it; most applications operate well below this threshold.

claim: GPT-4.1 has better instruction following than prior models, evaluated via new internal benchmark measuring format-following, negative instructions, and overconfidence
actionable: For instruction-following-heavy tasks (structured output, tool calling), test GPT-4.1; it may outperform older OpenAI models on these specific dimensions.

claim: GPT-4.1's max output tokens capped at 32,000 (same as 4o), missing opportunity for long-context generation unlike reasoning models with 64k-128k output
actionable: If you need long-form generation (extended writing, detailed reports), don't assume GPT-4.1 is better; check max output token limits.

claim: GPT-4.1 is a drop-in replacement for GPT-4o with better coding (SWE-Bench), instruction following, and lower cost, but deprecates GPT-4.5
actionable: For new projects, use GPT-4.1 instead of 4o; for existing 4.5 users, migrate to 4.1 for better value.
